free_text: Measurement of neutron activation cross sections for
major elements of water, air and soil between 30 and
AVF Cyclotron of the Cyclotron and
natural Li target. 5 % energy loss in target.
Neutron energy spectrum was measured using TOF method
Obtained neutron spectra are given on Fig.2 of
energy  thickness  neutron   neutron        of peak
Samples were placed within collimator, 250 cm from
1 hour to measure short half-life radionuclides,
scintillation detector, placed at 11 m from Li target
To measure gammas from sample after irradiation
Activation reaction rates were estimated by the peak
counts of gamma-ray spectra, the peak efficiency of
Ratio of peak reaction rate from Emin to Emax and the
total reaction rate from Eth to Emax - peak-to total
ratio of reaction rate - was estimated using measured
neutron flux and cross-section data of ENDF/B-VI.
For self-absorption of gamma-rays in samples,
for beam current fluctuation during irradiation,
for contribution of low-energy tail neutrons.
The following error components are included:
error of peak area estimation for positron
error of correction for contribution of
